spec_match.rationale: The cacheable probe now loads from a same-origin URL under `/v149/inline-script-cache/cold-vs-warm/probe`, keeps the network isolation key stable, and serves deterministic inline script source keyed by SHA-256 rather than a fresh `blob:` document.

interactive.rationale: The page provides live controls for a stable probe, a one-byte mutated probe, an eval-disqualified probe, and reset. Each run appends measured iframe timing, script-runtime marks, hash, route, and cache eligibility verdicts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and notice now describe what the browser exposes honestly: same-origin repeated loads, content-hash comparison, stable network isolation key, and PerformanceObserver script-* mark/measure readout rather than a direct cache-hit bit.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers the important edge cases: one literal changes from A to B and creates a new cold hash, the probe URL is same-origin instead of blob, and a separate eval-disqualified variant is never labelled as warm-cacheable.

references.rationale: The page keeps the required ChromeStatus link for feature 5132993816559616 and links to the feature index, cache-key probe, size-threshold explorer, and V8 code caching article.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, scoped button variants that set both background and color, no raw color literals, no inline event handlers/styles, and an overflow-contained responsive table for narrow screens.

summary: Cold vs Warm now exercises a real same-origin inline-script probe instead of blob iframes, removes dynamic code from the cacheable path, surfaces PerformanceObserver script marks, and includes the one-byte mutation plus eval-disqualification edge cases.
